We   use a perfect tense, not a present tense, to say how  long something has been 8  how     long?     present      tenses     not   used The tank contains about 7,000 litres at the moment.

Do you believe what he says? (     Are you believing... ?) not I like this wine, (     Pm -liking this wine.) meaning is 'just around now’ .

Some verbs are not generally used in progressive forms (►  4), even if the 7 verbs     not   generally       used    in  progressive         forms Your parents live in North London, don't they?

- My sister's living at home for the moment.

Chetford Castle stands on a hill outside the town, (     . . .-is standing...) - Why is that girl standing on the table?

'What do bears eat?* 'Everything.' (     -What are bears eating?-.. . ɔ - Look - the cat's eating your breakfast!

situations, or about things that happen regularly, repeatedly or all the time.

We   do not normally use the present progressive to talk about permanent 6 things     that    happen      all  the   time:   present      progressive        not   used Come and see us next week if you're passing through London.

What are you doing tomorrow evening?

We   often use the present progressive to talk about the future (►   36).

5 talking     about     the   future That child's getting bigger every day. House prices are going up again.

We   also use the present progressive to talk about developments and changes.

Why is he hitting the dog? I'm travelling a lot these days.

happening around the present (for more details, ►    34.1).

The  present progressive can refer to repeated actions and events, if these are just 3 repeated       actions Section3 Present     Tenses grammar •  34   present tenses: advanced points The universe is expanding and has been since its beginning.

The climate is getting warmer. (     The climate gets warmer.) very long-lasting.

We   use the present progressive for changes and developments, even if these are 2  long-lasting        changes You look lovely when you're smiling.

At seven, when the mail comes, I'm usually having breakfast.

actions and events.

But we  can use the present progressive for things that are going on around these I go to the mountains about twice a year.

Water boils at 100°  Celsius. (     Water is boiling at 100 Celsius.) not closely connected to the moment of speaking.

We   normally use the simple present for repeated actions and events which  are Why is he hitting the dog?  Jake's seeing a lot of Felicity these days.

happening around the moment of speaking.
